title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miranda Butler tells Hercule Poirot and Michael Garfield that she is searching for a legendary wishing well in the forest near the old quarry. She explains that Grandma Goodbody, whom she refers to as a witch, claims the well was sealed years ago because it was dangerous, possibly following an incident where a child named Kitty fell in. Garfield disputes the legend, suggesting the story was made up by Mrs. Goodbody. He informs Poirot that while there is a regular well in White Hill, the actual legend involved a "wishing tree"—a beech tree on a hill—that was split by lightning six years ago.

After Miranda departs, Poirot and Garfield discuss Garfield's professional dissatisfaction. A designer of houses and gardens, Garfield expresses a desire to move, possibly to Greece, because clients like Drayton insist on impractical or showy designs rather than following his professional recommendations.

The conversation shifts to the sudden death of Leslie Firle, a junior lawyer from a Manchester firm. Garfield explains that Leslie was killed with a knife, and local speculation suggests the motive was a jealous affair with a woman named Sandra. Poirot inquires about Olga, suggesting a connection between her and Leslie. Garfield confirms that Leslie and Olga were seen together frequently, though Olga is cautious and has not confided in him. Garfield notes that Leslie was a charming man involved with several women, which caused distress among his girlfriends. Poirot expresses interest in investigating the events and "unsavory things" that occurred before Leslie and Olga met.
